---
title: "Home"
site: workflowr::wflow_site
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: false
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

Welcome to my workflowR documentation of the CryoZoo project, a biobank of
live Tetrapod cell lines profiled with a diverse set of genomics methods.

This site walks through the species-selection and inventory pipeline, in
order:

1. [Clean CryoZoo Lists](Clean_CryoZoo_Lists.html) — ingest and clean the
   master species and sample lists.
2. [Update Cryostock](update_Cryostock.html) — update the cryotube storage
   inventory.
3. [Update Cell Culture](update_Cell_culture.html) — summarise cell culture
   and thawing outcomes.
4. [Update Extractions](update_Extractions.html) — summarise nucleic-acid
   extraction records.
5. [Update Sequencing](update_Sequencing.html) — aggregate sequencing status
   across library types.
6. [Update Sample Table](Update_Sample_table.html) — join everything into a
   master sample table.
7. [CryoZoo Summary Plots](plot_Cryozoo_summary.html) — summary figures for
   the collection.
8. [Mammal Tree](plot_Tree_mammal.html) — phylogenetic tree of CryoZoo mammal
   species.
9. [Bird Tree](plot_Tree_birds.html) — phylogenetic tree of CryoZoo bird
   species.

A lighter-weight alternative to steps 8–9 is also available:
[Simple Phylogenetic Trees](plot_trees_simple.html) — circular trees per
taxonomic group built from the Open Tree of Life, with an IUCN status ring.

See [analysis/README.md](https://github.com/lewange/cryozoo-overview/blob/master/analysis/README.md)
for the full pipeline description, including inputs, outputs, and package
dependencies for each step.
